title: Fix auto-resume after compaction and output token limit
Add pre-compaction custom instructions support in pi core
description: Pi core session_before_compact exposes customInstructions in the event, but SessionBeforeCompactResult only supports cancel/compaction and current agent-session.js does not pass extension-provided instructions into compact(). Add core support so extensions can preserve task context for reason=overflow compactions without replacing the whole summary.

title: Inject task-preservation instructions into overflow compaction
description:
## Problem
The PROACTIVE compact passes excellent customInstructions ("Preserve the current task, recent code changes, pending work, exact next step..."). But when pi-coding-agent's core triggers reason="overflow" on its own (the 413 / prompt-too-long path), the harness never injects instructions — overflow compactions get DEFAULT summarization, which loses task continuity.

Overflow is the worst time to lose context: it's mid-task, mid-tool-execution.

The Role Model's reactive path preserves task context via recompaction metadata.

## Fix
Add a session_before_compact listener (confirmed: NONE exists today — grep session_before_compact index.ts returns empty):

```ts
pi.on("session_before_compact", (event, ctx) => {
  if (event.reason === "overflow" || event.reason === "threshold") {
    event.customInstructions =
      "This compaction was triggered by context overflow mid-task. " +
      "Preserve: the current task, exact next step, recent code edits, " +
      "any unresolved errors, and pending tool operations. " +
      "After compaction, continue seamlessly without asking the user to resume.";
  }
});
```

## Verify first
Check whether SessionBeforeCompactEvent.customInstructions is settable from an extension (type shows optional; may be read-only or require a different mutation path via `preparation`). If read-only, use whatever before-phase override the core supports.

## Files
- EDIT: `index.ts` (new pi.on("session_before_compact", ...) handler)
- REF: node_modules/@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent/dist/core/extensions/types.d.ts (SessionBeforeCompactEvent)

## Acceptance
- On real context overflow, compaction summary preserves the in-flight task + next step
- Manual compact unaffected
- Biggest quality win — closes the gap vs the Role Model's reactive context preservation
close_reason: Not implementable in harness with current core API; superseded by pi-harness-runtime-zf0

title: Manual /compact triggers unwanted auto-resume (UX bug)
description:
## Bug
A user-initiated `/compact` (pause-to-think) gets auto-resumed by the harness, so the agent keeps going against the user's intent.

## Root cause
`index.ts:683` session_compact handler calls `shouldQueuePostCompactionResume(event, ...)` which returns true whenever `willRetry !== true`. A MANUAL compact has `willRetry=false` and no pending turn -> harness injects "resume" -> agent continues.

Reference agents (Role Model) NEVER auto-continue a user-initiated compact.

Confirmed: zero uses of `event.reason` in the harness today.

## Fix
Use the `reason: "manual" | "threshold" | "overflow"` field the core already provides:

In `proactive-compact.ts` shouldQueuePostCompactionResume:
```ts
export function shouldQueuePostCompactionResume(
  event: { willRetry?: boolean; reason?: "manual" | "threshold" | "overflow" },
  hasPendingMessages: boolean,
  options?: { force?: boolean },
): boolean {
  // Never auto-resume a manual compact — the user is in control.
  if (event.reason === "manual" && !options?.force) return false;
  return (options?.force === true || event.willRetry !== true) && !hasPendingMessages;
}
```

Then update `index.ts:683` session_compact handler to pass `event.reason` through (currently dropped).

## Files
- EDIT: `proactive-compact.ts` (shouldQueuePostCompactionResume signature + body)
- EDIT: `index.ts:683` (pass event.reason)

## Acceptance
- `/compact` typed manually -> agent does NOT auto-resume, waits for user
- reason="overflow" with willRetry=false -> still auto-resumes (regression guard)
- reason="threshold" -> still auto-resumes
- Depends on: pi-harness-runtime-r53 (tests must cover this case)
close_reason: Manual compact now blocks post-compaction auto-resume unless forced

title: Add tests for proactive-compact gate functions
description:
## Problem
`proactive-compact.ts` exports 4 pure gate functions that control ALL auto-resume behavior, but they have ZERO tests:
- `shouldTriggerProactiveCompact`
- `isOutputLimitAssistantMessage`
- `shouldQueueOutputLimitResume`
- `shouldQueuePostCompactionResume`

These are the single point of failure for "does the agent continue or stall after compaction / output limit". The Role Model's equivalents (threshold/warning-state calculators) are heavily tested.

## Scope
Add `proactive-compact.test.ts` covering:
- Manual compact -> shouldQueuePostCompactionResume returns false (UX bug guard)
- reason="overflow" + willRetry=false -> returns true
- Output-limit at attempt 3 with pending messages -> false
- stopReason: "length" / "max_output_tokens" / "max_tokens" detection
- errorMessage regex fallback ("reached the maximum output token limit")
- threshold gating (percent null -> false, >= 0.9 -> true)

## Why priority 2 / unblocker
The other fixes (manual-compact UX fix, overflow instructions) modify these exact functions. Tests must land first so those changes are safe.

## Files
- NEW: `proactive-compact.test.ts`
- REF: `proactive-compact.ts` (4 exported functions)

## Acceptance
- `bun test proactive-compact` passes
- All gate functions have >=3 cases each (true/false/edge)

title: Add escalating context-usage warning tiers in turn_end
description:
## Problem
Only the always-on footer status + the 90% trigger exist. No escalating amber/red alerts. The Role Model shows warnings at ~83% (threshold - 20k), errors higher.

## Fix
Add a turn_end check (index.ts:676) that surfaces escalating ctx.ui.notify warnings:

```ts
const TIER_WARNING = 0.75;  // amber
const TIER_ERROR = 0.85;    // red
// in turn_end:
const pct = ctx.getContextUsage()?.percent;
if (pct !== null && pct !== undefined) {
  if (pct >= TIER_ERROR) ctx.ui.notify(`Context at ${Math.round(pct)}% — approaching limit`, "warning");
  else if (pct >= TIER_WARNING) ctx.ui.notify(`Context at ${Math.round(pct)}%`, "info");
}
```

Add per-session dedup so it doesn't fire every turn (e.g., only when crossing into a higher tier).

## Files
- EDIT: `index.ts` (turn_end handler)

## Acceptance
- Single amber notify at 75%, single red at 85% (no spam)
- No notify above 90% (proactive compact handles it)
- Polish item — lowest priority
